# Carat Capital

> The trade paper of the jewelry world — original, sourced daily reporting on diamonds, gold and precious metals, colored gemstones, watches, jewelry auctions, and jewelry retail and technology. Published at https://caratcapital.org. All articles are original writing with named, linked sources; prices are dated and indicative.

## The paper
- [Front page](https://caratcapital.org/): today's edition, the wire, and the live price tape
- [The Record](https://caratcapital.org/the-record.html): a dated, sourced week-by-week chronicle of the industry
- [The Almanac](https://caratcapital.org/almanac.html): the quarter's key numbers in sourced tables (metals, exports, prices, auctions, retail)
- [The Field Guide](https://caratcapital.org/field-guide.html): plain-language introduction to how the jewelry trade works
- [About & editorial standards](https://caratcapital.org/about.html)
- [RSS feed](https://caratcapital.org/feed.xml)
- [Morning Brief newsletter](https://caratcapital.beehiiv.com): free daily email, 06:30 ET

## The six desks
- [Diamonds](https://caratcapital.org/diamonds.html): the stone that runs on trust
- [Gold & Metals](https://caratcapital.org/gold-metals.html): the metal beneath every margin
- [Colored Gemstones](https://caratcapital.org/gemstones.html): supply lines drawn in emerald, ruby and sapphire
- [Watches](https://caratcapital.org/watches.html): haute horlogerie, priced by the second
- [Auctions & Estates](https://caratcapital.org/auctions.html): where the trade marks itself to market
- [Retail & Technology](https://caratcapital.org/retail-tech.html): the counter, rebuilt for the next generation

## Recent articles
- [Plus 11.2%: June hands Swiss watchmaking its month back](https://caratcapital.org/a-plus-eleven-two-in-june.html): Exports reached CHF 2.4 billion in June on 1.3 million pieces — the strongest month of the year. America added 12.7%, France more than doubl
- [£133 million, up 76%: Watches of Switzerland answers its doubters](https://caratcapital.org/a-wos-133-million-answer.html): Statutory pre-tax profit jumped 76% to £133 million ($170 million) for the year — six weeks after the board waved off interest at a reported
- [33,000 carats after the end: Diavik sweeps its own floor](https://caratcapital.org/a-last-carats-at-diavik.html): Rio Tinto's Canadian mine closed in March. The second quarter still yielded 33,000 carats from remaining ore — down 97% — and first-half out
- [1,214 jobs on the table: the union answers Venetia's pause](https://caratcapital.org/a-twelve-hundred-at-venetia.html): South Africa's National Union of Mineworkers says De Beers' two-year halt puts 1,134 mine workers and 80 sales staff into Section 189A retre
- [Gold idles at $4,008: one quiet week left before the Fed speaks](https://caratcapital.org/a-quiet-week-at-four-thousand.html): Kitco's Monday board closed at $4,000.30, down 0.42%; Tuesday's early tape reads $4,008, near flat. Silver held $56.36 after leading Monday 
- [Up 8.6%, in fewer boxes: America's half-year at the jewelry counter](https://caratcapital.org/a-more-dollars-fewer-boxes.html): US jewelry revenue grew 8.6% in the first half and 13% in June, per Tenoris — but the average ticket rose 19% while unit sales fell. Bracele
- [Same-store, plus 39%: Hong Kong's gold counters run hot](https://caratcapital.org/a-gold-counters-run-hot.html): Luk Fook's June quarter: retail value +32%, gold product sales +50% same-store — while diamond sales fell 17%, and 58% on the mainland. Chow
- [Four times the estimate: the pearl keeps proving the trade wrong](https://caratcapital.org/a-pearls-print-a-quiet-win.html): Tenoris counts pearl jewelry among the surprises of America's first half; at Bonhams London this month, a two-row natural strand made £39,68
- [Four thousand, defended: gold walks into Fed week on its floor](https://caratcapital.org/a-four-thousand-defended.html): Gold closed Friday up $42.10 at $4,017.30 after touching $3,959, and opened Monday at $4,018. December hike odds sit near 82%, Brent crude a
- [Up 26.5% in a month: India's export machine restarts](https://caratcapital.org/a-india-ships-the-turn.html): Gems and jewellery exports rose 26.5% in June to $2.21 billion, per GJEPC data. Gold jewellery did the heavy lifting at +54.5%; cut-and-poli
- [Botswana answers Penny: partner, pre-empt, or bring a friend](https://caratcapital.org/a-gaborone-answers-penny.html): Minister Moeti Mohwasa told parliament the state's 15% of De Beers carries 'complete freedom' — join the Penny consortium, exercise pre-empt
- [Carbon-neutral, sanction-locked: Alrosa's green crown has no throne](https://caratcapital.org/a-greenest-stones-locked-out.html): TÜV Austria has certified all 29.8 million carats of Alrosa's 2025 output carbon-neutral, a second straight year. The footprint improved 60%
- [Fifteen tonnes of steel, and a conscience: Oris does the math](https://caratcapital.org/a-oris-counts-in-tonnes.html): Oris mills about 15 tonnes of steel a year — ten cars' worth — so CEO Rolf Studer counts the real footprint in travel and shipping. New pack
- [Berkshire's jeweler splits the job: revenue here, factories there](https://caratcapital.org/a-richline-splits-the-till.html): Richline promotes Rio Grande president Arien Gessner to chief revenue officer across four divisions and hands 30-year veteran Moss Makhoulia
- [Only 30% haven't: the counter quietly adopts AI](https://caratcapital.org/a-only-thirty-percent-havent.html): ONS survey data says just 30% of UK retailers haven't touched AI. Of the users, 46% put it on operations, 25.4% on personalisation — and 10.
